name: Budapest
ai_summary: Budapest, the capital of Hungary, provides a range of accessibility options across its public transport and major attractions. Over 90% of buses are low‑floor and many metro stations have elevators, while key sites such as the Parliament and Buda Castle offer wheelchair‑friendly entrances and restrooms. However, some historic venues (e.g., the M1 metro line and certain older buildings) still lack barrier‑free access. Overall, the city offers solid but not complete accessibility.
